source_pr.title: br: fix some unstable unit test cases.
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary:
Close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/30448
1. mock.start() failed due to conflict port. https://ci.pingcap.net/blue/organizations/jenkins/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/detail/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/24748/pipeline/
2. trace_serial_test failed due to schedule not stable in 10ms.

### What is changed and how it works?
1. add 10 times retry to get avaiable port.
2. change 10ms wait to 100ms
### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test


### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Mock cluster start sometimes failed due to port conflicts when allocating ports for the mock HTTP endpoints.
Trace test timing was unstable because it relied on very short fixed sleeps (10ms) and was sensitive to scheduling/CI load.
failure_signature: Port allocation conflict for mock cluster start, and timing-sensitive trace assertions due to too-short sleeps.
root_cause_explanation: Two instability sources were addressed: (1) mock cluster start could fail due to port conflicts when allocating/listening on ports; (2) the tracing test relied on very short `time.Sleep(10ms)` windows that were not stable under scheduling/CI load. The PR stabilizes the suite by retrying/validating port availability during mock cluster startup and by increasing the sleep duration (10ms → 100ms) and updating the timing assertions accordingly.
fix_pattern: Use robust resource allocation for ports (retry/validate availability) and avoid too-short sleeps by increasing timing windows for async operations.

evidence: evidence: br: fix some unstable unit test cases.
